CREATE TABLE employees (id INTEGER, department VARCHAR, salary BIGINT);
SELECT department, id, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY department ORDER BY salary DESC) AS row_num FROM employees;
CREATE TABLE departments (dept_id INTEGER, dept_name VARCHAR);
SELECT d.dept_name, e.id, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY d.dept_name ORDER BY e.salary DESC) AS row_num FROM employees e JOIN departments d ON e.department = d.dept_name;
CREATE TABLE sales (sales_id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, sales_amount BIGINT);
SELECT s.employee_id, e.salary, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY s.sales_amount DESC) AS sales_rank FROM sales s JOIN employees e ON s.employee_id = e.id;
CREATE TABLE performance (employee_id INTEGER, year INTEGER, score INTEGER);
SELECT p.year, e.department, e.id, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY p.year ORDER BY p.score DESC) AS position FROM performance p JOIN employees e ON p.employee_id = e.id;
SELECT dept_name, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (ORDER BY dept_name) AS department_rank FROM departments;
SELECT id, department, salary, row_number() OVER (ORDER BY salary DESC) AS salary_rank FROM employees WHERE salary > 65000;
CREATE TABLE projects (project_id INTEGER, employee_id INTEGER, project_name VARCHAR);
INSERT INTO projects VALUES (1, 1, 'Project A'), (2, 2, 'Project B'), (3, 3, 'Project C');
SELECT p.project_name, e.id, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY e.department ORDER BY p.project_id) AS project_rank FROM projects p JOIN employees e ON p.employee_id = e.id;
